title: KML
documentation:
## Purpose
KML (Keyhole Markup Language) is an XML-based format for expressing geographic annotation and visualization within maps and Earth browsers. The [KML](https://capdag.com/browse/media:kml;textable) media type represents `.kml` files conforming to the OGC KML standard.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes geographic data intended for Google Earth, Google Maps, or other KML-capable viewers — for example, annotated maps, tour paths, or overlay layers with styling information. For simpler vector data interchange without styling, [GeoJSON](https://capdag.com/browse/media:geojson;record;textable) is often a lighter alternative.

## Notes
KML files are UTF-8 XML with namespace `http://www.opengis.net/kml/2.2`. The `;textable` tag indicates the file is human-readable. Key elements include `Placemark`, `Folder`, `GroundOverlay`, and `NetworkLink`. Implementers should note that KML uses longitude-latitude order (not latitude-longitude) in coordinate tuples, and optional altitude is interpreted relative to the `altitudeMode` element.
